courses: Coursework: Computer Vision, Computer Networks, Machine Learning, Database Systems, Game Dev

work.name: AMI Lab, University of Michigan
highlights: Project 1: Practical Moiré-Based Visualization of Airflows with Commodity Cameras

Developed a Moiré-based Schlieren sensing framework that uses wallpaper patterns and low-cost cameras to visualize otherwise invisible heat plumes and gas-like flows in real kitchens, including an AutoMoiré calibration module and end-to-end pipelines for cooking-related ML tasks.
Project 0: Wearable IMU-based Activity Recognition and Motion Transcription

Explored wearable IMU-based activity recognition and motion transcription by surveying prior work, reproducing representative baselines, and prototyping transformer-based IMU localization that supports flexible device configurations.

work.name: DIDIS Lab, Shanghai Jiao Tong University
highlights: Worked on transformer-based motion planning, integrating geometric and collision-aware loss terms and building visualization tools to analyze how attention over obstacles affects path feasibility and trajectory quality.

awards.title: First Prize, 13th SJTU "Liming Cup" Mechanical Innovation Competition For Freshmen
UM–SJTU Joint Institute Outstanding Freshman Scholarship
